name: Document Data Extraction with easybits Extractor

parameters.content:
## 📥 Document Upload
Hosts a simple web form with a single file upload field. Users can upload any supported document (PDF, PNG, JPEG) directly through their browser.

## 🤖 Data Extraction
Sends the uploaded file to the easybits Extractor pipeline. Returns structured data under `json.data`, following the schema you defined in your pipeline mapping.

# 📄 easybits Extractor – Document Data Extraction

## What This Workflow Does
Upload a document (PDF, PNG, or JPEG) via a hosted web form. The file is sent to the **easybits Extractor**, which extracts structured data based on your defined schema and returns it as clean JSON.

## How It Works
1. **Form Upload** – A user uploads a file through the n8n web form
2. **Extraction** – The easybits Extractor processes the file and returns structured data under `json.data`

---

## Setup Guide

### 1. Create Your easybits Extractor Pipeline
1. Go to [extractor.easybits.tech](https://extractor.easybits.tech), sign up and click **"Create a Pipeline"** on your dashboard
2. Fill in the **Pipeline Name** and **Description** – describe the type of document you're processing
3. Upload a **sample document** as your reference
4. Click **"Auto-Mapping"** – the Extractor analyzes your sample document and automatically suggests fields and descriptions for you
5. Review the suggested fields, adjust any descriptions if needed, and click **"Save & Test Pipeline"** in the test tab to verify that the extraction works correctly
6. Go to **Pipeline Details → View Pipeline** and copy your **Pipeline ID** and **API Key**

> 💡 **Tip:** The auto-mapping gets you up and running in seconds. But the more specific your field descriptions are, the more accurate your results will be.

### 2. Install the easybits Extractor Node
1. The node is **verified** and works on n8n Cloud out of the box – just search for "easybits Extractor" in the node panel
2. For self-hosted instances, go to **Settings → Community Nodes → Install** and enter `@easybits/n8n-nodes-extractor`

### 3. Configure the Node
1. Add the **easybits Extractor** node to your workflow
2. Enter your **Pipeline ID** and **API Key** from the pipeline details page – that's it

The node reads binary data directly from the previous node in your workflow. No Base64 conversion or manual HTTP configuration needed.

### 4. Activate & Test
1. Click **Active** in the top-right corner of n8n
2. Open the form URL and upload a test document
3. Check the execution output
